MEYERS, NAVE, RIBACK & WEST
MICHAEL R. NAVE
STEVEN H. MEYERS
NATALIE E. WEST
ELIZABETH H. SILVER
MICHAEL S. RIBACK
MOLLY T. TAMI
MICHAEL F. RODRIGUEZ
KATHLEEN FAUBION
FREDERICK S. ETHERIDGE
WENDY A. ROBERTS

A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION

PENINSULA OFFICE

GATEWAY PLAZA
777 DAVIS STREET, SUITE 300
SAN LEANDRO, CALIFORNIA 94577
(415) 351-4300
FAX: (415) 351-4481

1220 홀로우 AVE., SUITE 250
BURLINGAME, CA 94010-4211
(415) 348-7130
FAX (415) 342-0866

MARIN OFFICE

1202 GRANT AVE., SUITE E
NOVATO, CA 94945
(415) 892-8878

REPLY TO

San Leandro

October 9, 1990

Ms. Kathy Donovan
Chief Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Request for Advice: Government Code § 83114(b)

Dear Ms. Donovan:

     The purpose of this letter is to request advice on behalf of
Carl Springer, a member of the City of Dublin Planning Commission.
Mr. Springer is also a traffic engineer by profession and,
commencing November 1, 1990, will be employed by TJKM, a traffic
engineering firm that serves as traffic engineer for the City of
Dublin pursuant to contract. The undersigned is the City Attorney
of the City of Dublin.

QUESTION

     Does Mr. Springer have a financial interest in decisions made
by him as a Planning Commission member where his employer, TJKM,
has prepared a traffic analysis or some other report for the City
of Dublin requiring Mr. Springer to disclose such interest and to
disqualify himself?

ANALYSIS

     As traffic engineer for the City, TJKM (a) prepares traffic
analyses for City staff's use in recommending the imposition of
traffic impact fees on applicants, (b) prepares traffic analyses
for use in preparation of environmental documents (EIRs, negative
declarations) on applications for development approvals and on
general plan amendments, and (c) otherwise responds to planning
staff inquiries regarding traffic impacts. TJKM's services to the
City of Dublin are pursuant to an agreement which provides for
compensation on an hourly basis.

To:        Kathy Donovan
Re:        Request for Advice: Government Code § 83114(b)
Date:      October 9, 1990
Page:      2


     I am advised by Mr. Springer that he will not initially be
working on any projects for TJKM that involve the City of Dublin.
He indicates that it is his understanding that every effort will
be made by TJKM not to assign him to work on any project involving
the City of Dublin (including the area within its sphere of
influence and extended planning area) but that it is possible that
such an assignment may be made.

     While TJKM will be a "source of income" to Mr. Springer, it
is unclear to us whether disqualification will be required. On the
one hand it would appear that Mr. Springer does not have a "finan-
cial interest" within the meaning of Code §§ 87100 and
87103 by virtue of the provisions of Regulation 18700.1(b)(4).
That Regulation appears to provide that an official is not
attempting to use his or her official position to influence a
governmental decision" if the official prepares submissions of an
engineering nature "to be used by a client in connection with a
proceeding before the agency."  However, this exception appears
aimed at the situation where an official is an engineer (or
employed by an engineering firm) preparing drawings or a submission
for a client, to be presented to the board on which the official
sits, rather than the situation here where the official's employer
prepares submissions for the city itself.  Does this exception
apply in this situation?  A second question arises regarding the
second sentence of subparagraph (4). If Mr. Springer receives the
agenda for the Planning Commission meeting which includes a traffic
report prepared by TJKM, can Mr. Springer contact the City planning
staff with questions regarding the report without losing the
exception of subparagraph (4)?

     On the other hand, Mr. Springer may be considered to have a
financial interest in a decision by virtue of the provisions of
Regulation 18702.1(a)(1) and (d). Subsection (a)(1) provides that
a person (including a business entity) that has been a source of
income to the official "is directly involved in a decision" if
there is a "nexus" between "the purpose for which the official
receive income and the governmental decision." Subsection (d)
defines "nexus" as being present "if the official receives income
to achieve a goal or purpose which would be achieved, defeated,
aided, or hindered by the decision." Again, it would appear that
this definition of "nexus" is geared more toward the situation
where an official receives income from an applicant for land use
entitlement, rather than where the official's income is derived
(through his employer) from the city for reports and analyses
prepared for purposes of evaluating applications.

To:        Kathy Donovan
Re:        Request for Advice: Government Code § 83114(b)
Date:      October 9, 1990
Page:      3


       Thank you for your prompt response to this inquiry.  Please
do not hesitate to contact me if you have any questions or need
additional information.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        MEYERS, NAVE, RIBACK & WEST

                                        [signature]

                                        Elizabeth H. Silver
                                        City Attorney
                                        City of Dublin

EHS:dlg

cc:  Larry Tong, Planning Director
     Richard Ambrose, City Manager
     Carl Springer, Planning Commissioner

MEYERS, NAVE, RIBACK & WEST

MICHAEL R. NAVE
STEVEN P. MEYERS
NATALIE E. WEST
ELIZABETH H. SILVER
MICHAEL S. RIBACK
MOLLY T. TAMI
MICHAEL F. RODRIGUEZ
KATHLEEN FAUBION
FREDERICK S. ETHERIDGE
WENDY A. ROBERTS

A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION

PENINSULA OFFICE

1220 HOWARD AVE., SUITE 250
BURLINGAME, CA 94010-4211
(415) 342-0886
FAX (415) 342-0886

GATEWAY PLAZA
777 DAVIS STREET, SUITE 300
SAN LEANDRO, CALIFORNIA 94577
(415) 351-4300
FAX: (415) 351-4481

MARIN OFFICE

1202 GRANT AVE., SUITE E
NOVATO, CA 94945
(415) 892-8878

November 21, 1990

REPLY TO:

San Leandro

Ms. Peggy Bernardy
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re:   Letter No. 90-644

Dear Ms. Bernardy:

     To date I have not received a response to my letter of October
9, received in your office on October 11, 1990.
Although I have telephoned you twice to find out the status of the
letter, I have not received a return telephone call.

     The City of Dublin requests a response to my letter on or
before December 3, 1990. There is at least one item scheduled for
the Planning Commission meeting of December 3, 1990, where the
City's traffic engineer, TJKM, has performed work. As you will
recall, one of the Commission members, Carl Springer, works for
TJKM. Due to one vacancy on the Commission and the anticipated
absence of one member on December 3, 1990, there will only be three
members present, including Mr. Springer. There will thus not be
a quorum present to consider the item in question if Mr. Springer
has to disqualify himself. In fairness to the applicant, we would
request a response before December 3.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        MEYERS, NAVE, RIBACK & WEST

                                        [illegible]

                                        Elizabeth H. Silver
                                        City Attorney, City of Dublin

EHS:dlg

cc:  Larry Tong, Planning Director
     Richard Ambrose, City Manager
     Carl Springer, Planning Commissioner
